title: Invariants surface lacks cross-collection set-equivalence primitive — can express 'item has at least one edge of type X' but not 'item-field-list-of-IDs matches edge-set-of-IDs exactly'
description: config.invariants[] today supports two invariant classes (per packaged samples/conception.json + active substrates): (1) requires-edge — assert that an item in a block (optionally filtered by a where clause on item fields) has at least one edge of one of N relation types in a specified direction; (2) status-consistency — assert that when an item in block X has status Y, a related item in block Z has status W. Neither class can express the cross-collection comparison invariants that arise once items carry structured array-fields with embedded IDs that should match edges elsewhere. Concrete instance discovered 2026-05-30 in the kills-tracking surface (commit e60d862): authority-docs item REF-001 carries surface_kills_mapping[] where each entry has implementing_feature_id (a reference to a FEAT-NNN item) and killed_friction_ids[] (a list of FB-NNN IDs the FEAT should kill). The intended audit is 'for each entry, the FEAT named by implementing_feature_id must have feature_depends_on_item edges to FB items matching killed_friction_ids exactly as a set.' Walking the entry's field-list + following a reference field + comparing two sets for equivalence are three operations the existing classes cannot perform. The closest existing rule (requires-edge) only confirms AT LEAST ONE matching edge exists; it cannot confirm 'edges match this specific list.'

evidence.reference: Packaged invariant definitions enumerate class=requires-edge and class=status-consistency only; no cross-collection class. Lived in current project's .project/config.json + .context-jit-spec-v2/config.json (same shape inherited).

Each of 4 entries carries implementing_feature_id pointing to a FEAT-NNN and killed_friction_ids[] listing FB-NNN IDs the FEAT should kill — the intended audit 'edges from implementing_feature_id match killed_friction_ids exactly' is the inexpressible invariant

Documents the limitation: 'authority-to-planning alignment invariant ... is not expressible in config.invariants[]'. resolved-friction-has-verification-edge invariant landed (completion side); authority-alignment invariant was named but not implementable in config.

Cross-collection / programmatic-walk validators can be registered at module-init via registerLensValidator({name, validate}). This is the existing code-level escape hatch — but it forces every cross-collection invariant into custom TypeScript, which is high friction + bypasses the config-declarable surface that simpler invariants enjoy

impact: Authority-to-planning alignment (kills-tracking) and other classes of cross-field consistency check are unenforceable as config-declared invariants. Other consumer patterns blocked by the same gap: feature.acceptance_criteria list should match the set of completed sub-tasks for that feature (currently no auto-check); decision.consequences list should align with verifications attesting each consequence (no auto-check); any 'this field's list of IDs should match this set of edges' pattern. Workaround is custom lens-validator TypeScript registered via registerLensValidator — works but forces every cross-collection rule into source code instead of config, raising the floor for substrate-author-declared invariants. The asymmetry undercuts pi-context's 'add an invariant, no code changes' value prop: simple invariants are config-declarable, cross-collection invariants require source edits. Substrate authors filing structured array-fields (which is increasingly common as kinds get richer) lose access to the config-declared invariant surface for the very invariants their data shape calls for.
canonical_vocabulary: invariant class; cross-collection invariant; set-equivalence; field-array-matches-edge-set; lens-validator; programmatic invariant; config-declarable invariant
proposed_resolution:
Add a new invariant class to the config.invariants[] schema (and the dispatch logic in pi-context's validator) that expresses cross-collection set-equivalence. Provisional shape:

class: "field-array-matches-edge-set"
block: <source-block-name>
field_path: <dotted path to the array field on items, e.g. "surface_kills_mapping">
entry_reference_field: <field on each array-entry that names the related item, e.g. "implementing_feature_id">
entry_value_field: <field on each array-entry that lists the IDs to match, e.g. "killed_friction_ids">
relation_type: <edge type to walk from entry_reference_field, e.g. "feature_depends_on_item">
direction: <as_parent|as_child>
match_mode: <exact-set-equivalence|subset|superset>
severity: <error|warning>
message: <templated, with {field_path entry index} + {missing IDs} + {extra IDs}>

Validator walks each item in <block>; for each entry in item[field_path]; resolves item identified by entry[entry_reference_field]; walks that item's edges of relation_type in direction; compares resulting child/parent ID set against entry[entry_value_field] per match_mode; emits diagnostic on mismatch naming the specific missing/extra IDs.

This enables the kills-tracking authority-alignment invariant directly:
  block: authority-docs
  field_path: surface_kills_mapping
  entry_reference_field: implementing_feature_id
  entry_value_field: killed_friction_ids
  relation_type: feature_depends_on_item
  direction: as_parent
  match_mode: exact-set-equivalence
  severity: error
  message: 'REF {id} surface_kills_mapping[{index}]: FEAT {implementing_feature_id} edge set does not match killed_friction_ids ({missing} missing; {extra} extra)'

Same class addresses feature.acceptance_criteria↔sub-task-completion and decision.consequences↔verifications patterns by parameterization without code.

Alternative considered + rejected: extend requires-edge with a 'count-or-set-match' parameter. Rejected because it overloads requires-edge's single-edge semantic (which is about presence, not set-shape) and would mix two semantically distinct invariant classes; clean separation as a new class is cheaper than overloading.

Work shape: schema addition to config.json invariants[] item shape; dispatcher case in lens-validator.ts (or wherever invariant evaluation lives) for the new class; test coverage; documentation in the conception.json's invariants[] description. Plan-mode implementation required per [[feedback_no_adhoc_dev]] + [[feedback_plan_mode_protocol]].
